scope_note: QA Nuclear Magic Spin-Extension Cert family [221]: adds Dirac fermion axiom D1 — sigma in {1,2} encodes spin alignment j = l + (2*sigma - 3)/2 with population 2j+1 = 2(e + sigma - 1). Identifies (b, e) = (n, l) with HO shell quantum N = 2b - e - 2. Fractional-1/2 promotion rule: when sigma=2 AND b=e+1 AND l >= l*, N_eff = N - 1/2 (intruder lands between HO shells). The 1/2 is the Dirac spin unit (derived from D1, not a free parameter). Threshold l* is derived from a single physics input P1: r = alpha/hbar_omega (nuclear spin-orbit-to-HO-gap ratio) in [1/3, 1/2). Then l* = ceil(1/r) = 3 by integer ceiling. Empirical nuclear r from Mayer-Jensen 1950 + Bohr-Mottelson 1969: r ~ 0.3-0.4, inside the window. Magic-shell criterion: N_eff in {0, 1, 2} OR N_eff half-integer. Cumulative population at magic closures: {2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, 126} — all 7 experimental nuclear magic numbers exactly, zero tunable parameters beyond one discrete physical ratio in a narrow integer-forcing window. Non-magic integer-N_eff closures at 40, 70, 112, 168 (HO residues) are physically smaller gaps, not observed as magic. Atomic analogue [220] needs no spin extension because atomic r ~ 0.01-0.02 never reaches l* = 1. Scope: combinatorial identity + one physical ratio input; distinct from [220] (axiomatic, zero physics), [218] (structural on given table), [219]/[217] (purely combinatorial). Source: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-13. Theory note: docs/theory/QA_NUCLEAR_MAGIC_SPIN_EXTENSION.md. Checks NMS_1+D1/HO/PROMOTION/THRESHOLD/MAGIC/P1/SRC/WITNESS/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
